item.title: 什么是浏览器？ | What is a browser?
item.description:
在您拥有的大多数设备中，无论是手机（安卓或 Mac）、平板电脑还是笔记本电脑，您都会发现安装了网络浏览器。 网络浏览器是我们用来浏览互联网的software 。 有 5 种主要浏览器： Chrome, Internet Explorer, Firefox, Safari和Opera 。 浏览器的主要功能是获取和显示我们从互联网（如 Facebook 或 youtube）请求的资源。 资源也可以是简单的图像、pdf 文件甚至是 JSON 文件。

每个浏览器都有一些共同的主要组件：

这是与用户交互的 GUI（ Graphical User Interface ）。 地址栏、后退/前进按钮、书签菜单等内容。

将其视为controller. 它由一些其他引擎（ rendering engine和javascript engine ）组成，用于在收到您的请求响应后渲染和设置您看到的网页的样式

负责网页的排版。 如果请求的内容是HTML和CSS文件， rendering engine就会知道用HTML和CSS解析响应并将结果显示给用户。 这是构建 DOM（ document object model ）和制作样式的地方。

大多数响应都带有一些javascript代码，用于创建动态组件或从 Web 获取其他内容。 渲染引擎和 JavaScript 引擎之间的主要区别在于对浏览器的依赖性。 渲染引擎与浏览器引擎紧密结合，另一方面，即使没有浏览器也可以使用 JavaScript 引擎。

用于绘制组合框和窗口等基本小部件。 该后端公开了一个非特定于平台的通用接口。 在它下面使用操作系统用户界面方法。

这是一个持久层。 浏览器可能需要在本地保存各种数据，例如 cookie。 浏览器还支持存储机制，例如 localStorage、IndexedDB、WebSQL 和 FileSystem

渲染引擎

呈现引擎的主要目的是在浏览器上向用户呈现（解析和显示）请求和接收的内容。

不同的浏览器使用不同的渲染引擎：Internet Explorer 使用 Trident，Firefox 使用 Gecko，Safari 使用 WebKit。 有关详细信息，请参阅webkit.org 。

渲染引擎将开始从网络层获取请求文档的内容。 这通常以 8kB 块的形式完成。

之后，这是渲染引擎的基本流程：

一旦暂停，zaf07 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，zaf07 将可以再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，zaf07 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 zaf07 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 zaf07 的方法：

降低 zaf07 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 zaf07 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

In most devices you have, be it a phone (android or mac), a tablet or a laptop, you will find a web browser installed. A web browser is a software we use to browse the internet. There are 5 major browsers: Chrome, Internet Explorer, Firefox, Safari and Opera. (maybe 4, excluding IE).

The browser’s main functionality is to fetch and display resources we request from the internet like Facebook or youtube. Resources can also be a simple image, pdf file or even a JSON file.

Each browser has a few main components common to each other:

1) User Interface

  • This are GUI (Graphical User Interface) users interact with. Stuff like the address bar, back/forward buttons, bookmarking menu etc..

2) Browser Engine

  • Think of this as the controller. It comprises of a few other engines (rendering engine & javascript engine) to render and style the webpage you see upon receiving the response of your request

3) Rendering engine

  • Responsible for the layout of the webpage. If the requested content was a HTML & CSS file, the rendering engine would know to parse the response with HTML & CSS and displays the result to the user. This is where the DOM (document object model) is constructed & styling is made.

4) Javascript Engine

  • Most response comes with some javascript code to create dynamic components or to fetch additional content from the web. The javasript engine is responsible for interpreting and executing the javascript code. The primary difference between a Rendering engine and a JavaScript engine lies around the dependency with browser. The rendering engine is tightly coupled with browser engine, on the other hand, a JavaScript engine can be worked upon even without a browser.

5) Networking

6) UI backend

  • used for drawing basic widgets like combo boxes and windows. This backend exposes a generic interface that is not platform specific. Underneath it uses operating system user interface methods.

7) Data Storage

  • This is a persistence layer. The browser may need to save all sorts of data locally, such as cookies. Browsers also support storage mechanisms such as localStorage, IndexedDB, WebSQL and FileSystem

The Rendering Engine

The rendering engine’s main purpose is to render (parse & display) the requested & received content to the user on the browser.

Different browsers use different rendering engines: Internet Explorer uses Trident, Firefox uses Gecko, Safari uses WebKit. Chrome and Opera (from version 15) use Blink, a fork of WebKit.

WebKit is an open source rendering engine which started as an engine for the Linux platform and was modified by Apple to support Mac and Windows. See webkit.org for more details.

The rendering engine will start getting the contents of the requested document from the networking layer. This will usually be done in 8kB chunks.

After that, this is the basic flow of the rendering engine:

1) Parsing HTML to construct DOM tree

2) Render tree construction

3) Layout of the render tree

4) Painting the render tree

Валентина Данилина - Jan 17

Andrii Melashchenko - Jan 17

Once suspended, zaf07 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, zaf07 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by zaf07 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If zaf07 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag zaf07:

Make all posts by zaf07 less visible

zaf07 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging zaf07 will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: Gli alberi di Merkle | Gli alberi di Merkle
item.description:
韦内里托詹马尔科

发表于 1 月 18 日

作品: radice di due foglie

组合功能

哈希（哈希（A + B）+哈希（C + D））

在抄本中：

 class MerkleTree { constructor(leaves, concat) { this.leaves = leaves; this.concat = concat; } getRoot() { this.root = this.concat(this.leaves[0], this.leaves[1]); return this.root; } } module.exports = MerkleTree;

层数倍增

默克尔酒厂

散列（AB，散列（C，D））

在 ogni strato 中，dovremo combinare gli elementi due alla volta fino a raggiungere uno strato con un solo elemento。 在 ogni caso 中，vediamo il processo mentale su come affrontare il problema。

无限：

  • 一个 ogni livello si controlla se è rimasto un solo elemento。 在 questo modo si saprà quando restituire la radice in base al numero di livelli su cui si è lavorato。

    案例 2: Gestire un numero dispari di foglie

    来票价？ 阿尔贝里的雷戈拉

    在抄本中：

     class MerkleTree { constructor(leaves, concat) { this.leaves = leaves; this.concat = concat; } // Funzione di combinazione combine(newLeaves, leafLength) { let n = leafLength; if (leafLength == 1) { // console.log(newLeaves) return newLeaves; } else { let i = 0; for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i += 2) { newLeaves[i / 2] = this.concat(newLeaves[i], newLeaves[i + 1]) } newLeaves[i / 2] = newLeaves[leafLength - 1]; return this.combine(newLeaves.slice(0, Math.ceil(leafLength / 2)), Math.ceil(leafLength / 2)) } } getRoot() { // this.root = this.concat(this.leaves[0], this.leaves[1]) // console.log(this.combine(this.leaves, this.leaves.length)) return this.combine(this.leaves, this.leaves.length) } } module.exports = MerkleTree;

    一旦暂停，gianmarcodev 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，gianmarcodev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，gianmarcodev 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 gianmarcodev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 gianmarcodev 的操作：

    降低 gianmarcodev 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 gianmarcodev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Venerito Gianmarco

Posted on Jan 18

Gli alberi di Merkle ci permettono di verificare se un dato è contenuto/compreso all'interno di una struttura di dati più grande, senza il vincolo di dover avere tutti i dati di quella struttura. Ciò significa che possono essere utilizzati per verificare le incoerenze in tutti i tipi di sistemi distribuiti, che è la funzione che ci interessa di più :)

Per le blockchain ed i sistemi distribuiti, la memorizzazione delle transazioni come alberi di Merkle ci permette di esaminare un blocco e verificare che una transazione ne faccia parte, disponendo solo di una parte dei dati!

Ricordate che i dati di un blocco blockchain sono, semplificando, un insieme di transazioni.

Vediamo un esempio.

Albero di Merkle ipotetico: ABCDEFGHIJ

In questo albero ogni lettera rappresenta un hash, e le lettere combinate rappresentano la concatenazione degli hash e l'hashing di questi ultimi.

      Radice 
        / \
    ABCD EFGHIJ
     | / \
    ABCD EFGH IJ
    / \ / \ |
   AB CD EF GH IJ
  / \ / \ / \ / \ / \      
  A B C D E F G H I J

Per dimostrare che l'hash A fa parte della Radice di Merkle non è necessario conoscere gli hash C o D, ma solo l'hash CD. La prova necessaria per A è:

🧠 > Hash(Hash(A + B) + CD) + EFGHIJ) ,

nella quale abbiamo bisogno di conoscere solo gli hash B, CD e EFGHIJ per dimostrare che A è nella radice di Merkle.

Se tutto questo vi sembra senza senso, non preoccupatevi :)

Presto uniremo i puntini ("looking backwards") e torneremo su questo argomento.

Combinare due foglie

Combinare due foglie
Bene, costruiamo un albero di Merkle!

Un albero di Merkle prende un array di nodi foglia, combinandoli insieme due alla volta, strato per strato, fino a ridurli a un singolo nodo radice. Questo forma una struttura ad albero di hashing.

Obiettivo: radice di due foglie

Per prima cosa, scriviamo un costruttore per la classe MerkleTree. Questo costruttore accetta due argomenti passati in quest'ordine:

1) Un array di nodi foglia;
2) Una funzione di combinazione utilizzata per concatenare e fare l'hash di due foglie;

Diamo un'occhiata più da vicino alla funzione di combinazione.

Funzione di combinazione

Per semplificare l'implementazione di questo albero di Merkle e per facilitarne il debug, passeremo una funzione di combinazione al costruttore MerkleTree.

Questa funzione prenderà due argomenti: un nodo foglia sinistro e un nodo foglia destro e restituirà la combinazione risultante.

Per esempio, in un albero a quattro foglie:

  Radice   
    / \    
   AB CD
  / \ / \ 
  A B C  D

La funzione viene utilizzata tre volte, per ogni combinazione. La indicheremo qui come Hash:

Hash(Hash(A + B) + Hash(C + D))

Per prima cosa combiniamo A e B. Poi combiniamo C e D. Infine combiniamo il risultato di queste due combinazioni AB e CD per ottenere l'hash della radice.

Confronteremo poi la stringa creata con il risultato atteso.

Aggiungiamo quindi una funzione getRoot alla classe MerkleTree. Questa funzione troverà la radice di Merkle.

Come detto prima, in questa fase è necessario prendere solo due foglie e metterle in hash insieme:

In questo esempio, A e B sono i nodi foglia e la radice è il risultato della combinazione. È sufficiente prendere il primo e il secondo nodo foglia e utilizzare la funzione di combinazione per ottenere il risultato.

Non ci preoccupiamo ancora di generalizzare il nostro codice: più avanti passeremo ad uno scenario più approfondito.

In codice:

class MerkleTree {
    constructor(leaves, concat) {
        this.leaves = leaves;
        this.concat = concat;
    }
    getRoot() {
        this.root = this.concat(this.leaves[0], this.leaves[1]);
        return this.root;
    }
}

module.exports = MerkleTree;

Strati multipli

Ora è il momento di gestire un albero di Merkle più grande!

Questo albero avrà più livelli. Ad esempio, con quattro nodi foglia si combineranno i primi due nodi e poi gli ultimi due. Quindi si prenderanno i due risultati e li si combinerà per ottenere il nodo radice.

🧠 > Prima di generalizzare l'algoritmo, potrebbe essere utile rivedere lo scopo dell'albero di Merkle.

Scopo dell'albero di Merkle

Consideriamo l'esempio delle quattro foglie:

  ABCD
    / \ 
   AB CD
  / \ / \
  A B C  D

Di cosa abbiamo bisogno per dimostrare che C è compresa in ABCD?

Si potrebbe dire che abbiamo bisogno di A, B e D. Ma in realtà queste sono più informazioni di quelle che ci servono!

In realtà abbiamo bisogno solo di AB e D, infatti:

Hash(AB, Hash(C, D))

In questa derivazione di ABCD, possiamo dimenticarci di A e B. Non abbiamo bisogno di sapere cosa sono per dimostrare che C è nella Radice. Abbiamo solo bisogno di D e AB.

Questa ottimizzazione è la forza degli alberi di Merkle. Diventa ancora più evidente con alberi più grandi, dove sono necessari meno dati per dimostrare che un nodo foglia fa parte dell'albero. Un albero Merkle di 128 nodi richiede solo altri 7 hash per dimostrare che un nodo fogliare appartiene all'insieme.

Aggiornarniamo ora la funzione getRoot per gestire alberi di Merkle con più di due nodi foglia.

Nella scomposizione della logica degli alberi di Merkle, prima otteniamo l'hash di A e B, poi di C e D. Poi passiamo all'hash della combinazione di A e B (AB) con la combinazione di C e D (CD). Qualcosa del genere:

  ABCD
    / \ 
   AB CD
  / \ / \
  A B C  D

Nella stesura del codice sarà utile pensare all'albero come se avesse più livelli:

1) Il primo strato è costituito dalle foglie (A, B, C, D). 2) Il secondo è la combinazione di entrambe le combinazioni (AB, CD).

3) L'ultimo strato è la combinazione finale: la radice di Merkle (ABCD).

In ogni strato, dovremo combinare gli elementi due alla volta fino a raggiungere uno strato con un solo elemento. A quel punto possiamo fermarci, sapendo di aver trovato la radice.

Approccio consigliato

Ci sono diversi modi per affrontare la scrittura di questo algoritmo. Innanzitutto, occorre scegliere tra ricorsione e iterazione. Personalmente, trovo l'approccio ricorsivo più elegante e facile da usare. Sentitevi liberi di scegliere l'approccio con cui vi sentite più a vostro agio :)

In ogni caso, vediamo il processo mentale su come affrontare il problema.

Abbiamo un albero di Merkle con un numero arbitrario di nodi foglia. Forse è un albero a quattro foglie:

  Radice
    / \ 
   AB CD
  / \ / \
  A B C D

Forse è l'albero magico dalle otto foglie:


      Radice
       / \
    ABCD EFGH
    / \ / \
   AB CD EF GH
  / \ / \ / \ / \
  A B C D E F G H

O forse ancora peggio lol

L'approccio consigliato per questo problema è quello di suddividerlo in livelli.

Consideriamo l'albero a otto foglie. Per ogni livello, vogliamo prendere 2 nodi e combinarli.

Quindi, se ci troviamo nel livello inferiore:

  • Combinare A e B per ottenere AB
  • Combinare C e D per ottenere CD
  • Combinare E e F per ottenere EF
  • Combinare G e H per ottenere GH

Dopodiché, saliamo di livello! Il prossimo livello:

  • Combinare AB e CD per ottenere ABCD
  • Combinare EF e GH per ottenere EFGH

Infine:

  • Combinare ABCD ed EFGH per ottenere ABCDEFGH

A ogni livello si controlla se è rimasto un solo elemento. Se rimane un solo elemento, abbiamo trovato la radice.

💡 > In alternativa, si può calcolare in anticipo quanti strati ci saranno nell'albero. In questo modo si saprà quando restituire la radice in base al numero di livelli su cui si è lavorato.

Caso 2: Gestire un numero dispari di foglie

Consideriamo ora il caso di un albero Merkle con un numero dispari di foglie.

Come fare? Possiamo adottare questa strategia: ogni volta che non abbiamo una coppia per un dato elemento, vogliamo semplicemente portare quella foglia "single" ad un livello più in alto:

   Radice
    / \ 
   AB  C
  / \  |
  A B  C

In questo caso non usiamo il nodo C finché non lo combiniamo con AB per creare la radice Merkle.
Gestiamo questa casistica nella nostra funzione getRoot.

La regola degli alberi dispari

La regola per gli alberi dispari è sempre quella di utilizzare tutto ciò che si trova sul lato sinistro prima di riempire il lato destro dell'albero.

Ipotizziamo un albero con cinque foglie: utilizziamo le prime quattro come lato sinistro e portiamo la quinta fino all'ultima combinazione.

   Radice
     /  \
    ABCD  E
    / \   |
   AB CD  E
  / \ / \ |
  A B C D E

In codice:

class MerkleTree {
    constructor(leaves, concat) {
        this.leaves = leaves;
        this.concat = concat;
    }
    // Funzione di combinazione
    combine(newLeaves, leafLength) {
        let n = leafLength;

        if (leafLength == 1) {
            // console.log(newLeaves)
            return newLeaves;
        }
        else {
            let i = 0;
            for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i += 2) {
                newLeaves[i / 2] = this.concat(newLeaves[i], newLeaves[i + 1])
            }
            newLeaves[i / 2] = newLeaves[leafLength - 1];
            return this.combine(newLeaves.slice(0, Math.ceil(leafLength / 2)), Math.ceil(leafLength / 2))
        }
    }

    getRoot() {
        // this.root = this.concat(this.leaves[0], this.leaves[1])
        // console.log(this.combine(this.leaves, this.leaves.length))
        return this.combine(this.leaves, this.leaves.length)
    }

}

module.exports = MerkleTree;

🧠💼 Unvalidated Ideas - Jan 17

Once unsuspended, gianmarcodev will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If gianmarcodev is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: JavaScript 中的“this”关键字 | "this" keyword in JavaScript
item.description:
在 JavaScript 中， this关键字存储对象的引用。 与许多其他语言不同，它不受 JavaScript 中类的限制，它的值在运行时进行评估。

在上面的示例中，当调用consumer.printDescription()时， this将存储调用该方法的对象的引用，在本例中为消费者，并将提供消费者对象中提供的描述。 同样，当调用producer.printDescription()时， this将引用生产者对象和控制台“Produces Something”。

在函数中使用“this”关键字

在非严格模式下，在这种情况下this的值将是全局window对象，并且可以通过this关键字访问窗口对象上存在的属性。

 window.description = "Description added in window object" function printDescription() { console.log( this.description ); } printDescription(); // Output:- "Description added in window object"

在严格模式下， this将是未定义的，上面的示例将在尝试访问未定义的描述时给出错误。

箭头函数中没有“this”

箭头函数没有自己的this ，如果我们在箭头函数内部use this ，那么它是从外部函数中获取的。

 const consumer = { description: "Consumes Something", printDescription: function () { const arrow = () => { console.log(this.description) } arrow(); } } consumer.printDescription(); // Output:- "Consumes Something"

在上面的例子中，consumer 调用了printDescription方法，因此printDescription里面的this就会有 consumer 对象的引用。

在printDescription方法中，当箭头函数被调用时， this将从当前具有“消费者”对象引用的外部函数中获取，并将导致存储在消费者对象中的描述值。

箭头函数的这一特殊功能有助于解决在嵌套函数调用的情况下丢失this关键字引用的问题。

 const consumer = { description: "Consumes Something", printDescription: function () { function fn() { console.log(this.description) } fn(); } } consumer.printDescription(); // Output:- undefined

当调用 printDescription 时， this将引用 consumer 对象，因为 consumer 正在调用printDescription方法，但是在printDescription方法中的fn函数内部， this将引用全局 window 对象，因为没有其他对象正在调用fn函数 which类似于我们在上面在函数部分使用“this”关键字中讨论的情况。

这会导致在嵌套函数调用中丢失消费者对象的引用，可以借助箭头函数来解决。

在嵌套函数调用中丢失“this”关键字的替代解决方案

有一种替代解决方案可以在不使用箭头函数的情况下将主对象的引用保留在嵌套调用中。

 const consumer = { description: "Consumes Something", printDescription: function () { const that = this; function fn() { console.log(that.description) } fn(); } } consumer.printDescription() // Output:- "Consumes Something"

我们可以将this关键字的值存储在某个变量中，并在嵌套函数调用中使用该变量代替this关键字。



原文:

In JavaScript, this keyword stores the reference of the object. Unlike in many other languages this is not bounded to a class in JavaScript, it's value is being evaluated during the run-time.

this keyword take the reference of the object who is calling the method.

const consumer = { description: "Consumes Something" }
const producer = { description: "Produces Something" }

function printDescription() {
   console.log( this.description );
}

// Same printDescription function is being used in both the Objects
consumer.printDescription = printDescription;
producer.printDescription = printDescription;

consumer.printDescription(); // Output:- "Consumes Something"
producer.printDescription(); // Output:- "Produces Something"

In the above example, when consumer.printDescription() is called , this will store the reference of the object who is calling the method, which is consumer in this case and it will give the description provided in the consumer object. Similarly when producer.printDescription() is being called, this will refer to the producer object and console "Produces Something".

Using "this" keyword in function

this keyword can even be used inside a function without using an object

function printDescription() {
   console.log( this.description );
}

printDescription(); // Output:- undefined

In non-strict mode, value of this in such cases will be the global window object and properties existing on the window object can be accessible via this keyword.

window.description = "Description added in window object"

function printDescription() {
   console.log( this.description );
}

printDescription(); // Output:- "Description added in window object"

While in strict mode, this will be undefined and above example will give an error for trying to access description on undefined.

No "this" in Arrow function

Arrow function do not have their own this, If we use this inside the arrow function, then it is taken from the outer function.

const consumer = {
   description: "Consumes Something",
   printDescription: function () {
       const arrow = () => { console.log(this.description) }
       arrow();
   }
}

consumer.printDescription(); // Output:- "Consumes Something"

In the above example, consumer calls the printDescription method, because of which this inside the printDescription will have the reference of consumer object.

In printDescription method, when arrow function is being called, this will be taken from the outer function which currently has the reference of "consumer" object and it will result in the description value stored inside consumer object.

This special feature of arrow function helps in resolving the issue of losing reference of this keyword in case of nested function calls.

const consumer = {
   description: "Consumes Something",
   printDescription: function () {
       function fn() { console.log(this.description) }
       fn();
   }
}

consumer.printDescription(); // Output:- undefined

When printDescription is called, this will have the reference of consumer object, because consumer is calling the printDescription method, but inside the fn function in printDescription method, this will have the reference of global window object because no other object is calling the fn function which is similar to the case which we discussed above in Using "this" keyword in function section.

This will lead to losing the reference of consumer object in the nested function call, which can be resolved with the help of arrow function.

Alternative solution for losing "this" keyword in nested function calls

There is an alternative solution to keep the reference of main object inside the nested calls without the use of arrow functions.

const consumer = {
   description: "Consumes Something",
   printDescription: function () {
       const that = this;
       function fn() { console.log(that.description) }
       fn();
   }
}

consumer.printDescription() // Output:- "Consumes Something"

We can store the value of this keyword in some variable and use that variable inside the nested function call in place of this keyword.

item.title: Solidity 中的事件：初学者指南 | Events in Solidity: A Beginner's Guide
item.description:
区块链技术有可能彻底改变广泛的行业，而以太坊区块链是构建去中心化应用程序 (dApps) 的最受欢迎的平台之一。 以太坊的一个关键特征是智能合约能够通过使用事件相互交互以及与外部客户交互。

在这篇文章中，我们将了解智能合约开发环境中的事件，以及如何使用它们来创建强大的 dApp。

那么，Solidity 中的事件是什么？

简单来说，事件是智能合约发出存储在区块链上的日志语句的一种方式。 它们可用于通知外部客户或其他合同更改或合同内采取的行动。

事件是使用“event”关键字定义的，可以包括任意数量的各种类型的参数。 它们可以在合约函数中使用“emit”关键字来发射。 发出事件时，它会在区块链上创建一个日志条目，其中包括事件的名称及其相关参数。

例如，假设我们有一个虚拟柠檬水摊的智能合约。 每次有人买一杯柠檬水，我们都想跟踪已售出多少杯。 我们可以使用事件来记录此信息。

 event CupSold(uint cupsSold);

每次客户购买一杯柠檬水时，我们都会递增一个变量以跟踪售出的杯子总数，然后“发出”事件，如下所示：

 function buyLemonade() public { cupsSold++; emit CupSold(cupsSold); }

这将创建一个日志，说明一个杯子已售出，并包括到那时为止已售出的杯子总数。 任何拥有合同代码副本的人都可以访问此信息，并可用于跟踪柠檬水摊的成功情况。

另一个例子是对于虚拟拍卖行，每次出售物品时，我们都想通知卖家他们的物品已经售出，并通知买家他们赢得了拍卖。

 event ItemSold(address seller, address buyer, uint price); event AuctionWon(address buyer, uint price);

每次拍卖结束并售出一件物品时，我们都会“发出”这两个事件，如下所示：

 function auctionEnd(address winner, uint finalPrice) public { emit ItemSold(msg.sender, winner, finalPrice); emit AuctionWon(winner, finalPrice); }

这将创建两个日志，一个日志表明一件商品已售出，包括卖家地址、买家地址和最终价格。 另一条日志将说明买家赢得了拍卖以及该物品的售价。

外部客户端或其他合约可以通过web3.js或ether.js或其他工具（如Truffle和Hardhat ）访问这些日志。 它们允许您监听和过滤智能合约发出的事件，并从区块链中检索事件。

作为区块链开发人员，了解如何在智能合约开发中有效使用事件非常重要。 事件是一种在智能合约中创建重要操作或数据更改日志的方法，这些日志可以存储在区块链上并可供外部客户或其他合约访问。

  • 在设计事件时，重要的是要考虑要在日志条目中包含哪些信息，以及外部客户或其他合同将如何使用这些信息。 您还应该考虑您正在记录的信息对隐私和安全的影响。
  • 要记住的一件事是事件存储在区块链上，任何人都可以访问。 这意味着如果您正在记录敏感信息，您应该采取措施确保它被正确加密或散列。
  • 要记住的另一件重要事情是事件与函数调用不同。 虽然函数调用允许智能合约相互交互，但事件用于创建外部客户端可以访问的日志条目。
  • 同样重要的是要注意事件可以被监听，这意味着外部客户端或其他合约可以对特定事件做出反应。 例如，外部客户端可以侦听“CupSold”事件并使用日志条目中提供的信息来更新柠檬水摊销售的可视化表示或触发某些操作。

    • 现在如何阅读这些发出的事件

    有几种方法可以从智能合约中读取发出的事件。 一种常见的方法是使用 web3.js 或 ether.js 之类的客户端，这是一个 JavaScript 库，可让您与以太坊区块链进行交互。

    • 该方法将合约地址、事件名称和选项对象作为参数。

    例如，要从柠檬水合同中检索所有过去的“CupSold”事件，您可以使用以下代码：

     const lemonadeContract = new web3.eth.Contract(lemonadeABI, lemonadeAddress); lemonadeContract.getPastEvents("CupSold", { fromBlock: 0, toBlock: "latest" }, (error, events) => { console.log(events); });

    这将返回一个对象数组，每个对象代表过去的“CupSold”事件，包括块号、交易哈希和事件参数的值。

    读取发出的事件的另一种常见方法是使用诸如以太坊区块浏览器之类的工具，它允许您在区块链上搜索和查看合约事件，而无需编写任何代码。

    您还可以使用像 Truffle 这样的工具，它是以太坊的开发环境、测试框架和资产管道，它允许您使用其内置的事件发射器 Truffle emits 来监听智能合约发出的事件。

    • 它可用于以类似于 web3.js 的方式读取智能合约发出的事件。

    下面是一个示例，说明如何使用 Ether.js 从柠檬水合约中检索所有过去的“CupSold”事件：

     const ethers = require('ethers'); const provider = new ethers.providers.JsonRpcProvider(); const contract = new ethers.Contract(lemonadeAddress, lemonadeABI, provider); contract.on("CupSold", (seller, buyer, price) => { console.log(`A cup of lemonade was sold for ${price} by ${seller} to ${buyer}`); });

    每当柠檬水合约发出“CupSold”事件时，这将向控制台记录一条消息。

    您还可以使用 contract.queryFilter() 函数来获取符合特定条件的所有事件。

     const filter = contract.queryFilter("CupSold", { fromBlock: 0, toBlock: "latest" }); filter.then((events) => { console.log(events) });

    这将返回一个对象数组，每个对象代表过去的“CupSold”事件，包括块号、交易哈希和事件参数的值。

    总之，您可以使用 web3.js 或 Ether.js 来读取智能合约发出的事件。 这两个库都允许您按块号过滤事件并按事件名称检索事件。 主要区别在于语法，但思想是相同的，检索智能合约发出的事件。

    同样重要的是要注意，web3.js 和 Ether.js 都允许您监听事件，因此您可以在事件发出时触发操作。 这对于创建实时响应事件的 dapp 很有用。



    原文:

    Blockchain technology has the potential to revolutionize a wide range of industries, and the Ethereum blockchain is one of the most popular platforms for building decentralized applications (dApps). One of the key features of Ethereum is the ability for smart contracts to interact with one another and with external clients through the use of events.

    In this post, we'll take a look at what events are in the context of smart contract development, and how they can be used to create powerful dApps.

    So, what are events in Solidity?

    In simple terms, events are a way for smart contracts to emit log statements that are stored on the blockchain. They can be used to notify external clients or other contracts of changes or actions taken within a contract.

    Events are defined using the "event" keyword, and can include any number of parameters of various types. They can be emitted using the "emit" keyword within a contract's function. When an event is emitted, it creates a log entry on the blockchain that includes the event's name and its associated parameters.

    For example, let's say we have a smart contract for a virtual lemonade stand. Every time someone buys a cup of lemonade, we want to keep track of how many cups have been sold. We can use an event to log this information.

    event CupSold(uint cupsSold);

    Every time a customer buys a cup of lemonade, we would increment a variable to keep track of the total number of cups sold and then "emit" the event, like this:

    function buyLemonade() public {
    cupsSold++;
    emit CupSold(cupsSold);
}

    This would create a log that says a cup was sold and includes the total number of cups sold up until that point. This information can be accessed by anyone who has a copy of the contract's code and can be used to track the success of the lemonade stand.

    Another example is for a virtual auction house, Every time an item is sold, we want to notify the seller that their item has been sold and notify the buyer that they have won the auction.

    event ItemSold(address seller, address buyer, uint price);
event AuctionWon(address buyer, uint price);

    Every time an auction ends and an item is sold, we would "emit" the two events, like this:

    function auctionEnd(address winner, uint finalPrice) public {
    emit ItemSold(msg.sender, winner, finalPrice);
    emit AuctionWon(winner, finalPrice);
}

    This would create two logs, one log that says an item was sold and includes the address of the seller, the address of the buyer, and the final price. And the other log will state that the auction was won by the buyer and the price that the item was sold for.

    These logs can be accessed by external clients or other contracts through web3.js or ether.js, or other tools like Truffle and Hardhat. They allow you to listen and filter events emitted by a smart contract and retrieve the events from the blockchain.

    As a blockchain developer, it is important to understand how to use events effectively in your smart contract development. The key thing to **keep in mind **is that :

    • Events are a way to create a log of important actions or data changes within a smart contract that can be stored on the blockchain and accessed by external clients or other contracts.
    • When designing an event, it is important to consider what information you want to include in the log entry, as well as how that information will be used by external clients or other contracts. You should also consider the privacy and security implications of the information you are logging.
    • One thing to keep in mind is that events are stored on the blockchain and can be accessed by anyone. This means that if you are logging sensitive information, you should take steps to ensure that it is properly encrypted or hashed.
    • Another important thing to keep in mind is that events are not the same as function calls. While function calls allow smart contracts to interact with one another, events are used to create log entries that can be accessed by external clients.
    • It's also important to note that events can be listened to, which means that external clients or other contracts can react to specific events. For example, an external client could listen for the "CupSold" event and use the information provided in the log entry to update a visual representation of the lemonade stand's sales or trigger some action.

    Now how to read these emitted events

    There are several ways to read emitted events from a smart contract. One common way is to use a client like web3.js or ether.js, which is a JavaScript library that allows you to interact with the Ethereum blockchain.

    • web3.js way Once you have web3.js set up and connected to the Ethereum network, you can use the "web3.eth.getPastEvents" method to retrieve past events from a contract. The method takes in the contract address, the event name, and an options object as arguments.

    For example, to retrieve all past "CupSold" events from the lemonade contract, you could use the following code:

    const lemonadeContract = new web3.eth.Contract(lemonadeABI, lemonadeAddress);
lemonadeContract.getPastEvents("CupSold", {
    fromBlock: 0,
    toBlock: "latest"
}, (error, events) => {
    console.log(events);
});

    This will return an array of objects, each object representing a past "CupSold" event, including the block number, transaction hash, and the values of the event's parameters.

    Another common way to read emitted events is by using a tool such as an Ethereum block explorer, which allows you to search for and view contract events on the blockchain without having to write any code.

    You can also use tools like Truffle which is a development environment, testing framework and asset pipeline for Ethereum, that allows you to listen to events emitted by your smart contract with their built-in event emitter, Truffle emits.

    • Ether.js way Ether.js is a JavaScript library similar to web3.js that allows you to interact with the Ethereum blockchain. It can be used to read events emitted by smart contracts in a similar way to web3.js.

    Here's an example of how you could use Ether.js to retrieve all past "CupSold" events from the lemonade contract:

    const ethers = require('ethers');
const provider = new ethers.providers.JsonRpcProvider();

const contract = new ethers.Contract(lemonadeAddress, lemonadeABI, provider);
contract.on("CupSold", (seller, buyer, price) => {
    console.log(`A cup of lemonade was sold for ${price} by ${seller} to ${buyer}`);
});

    This will log a message to the console every time a "CupSold" event is emitted by the lemonade contract.

    You can also use the contract.queryFilter() function to get all events that match certain criteria.

    const filter = contract.queryFilter("CupSold", {
    fromBlock: 0,
    toBlock: "latest"
});
filter.then((events) => {
    console.log(events)
});

    This will return an array of objects, each object representing a past "CupSold" event, including the block number, transaction hash, and the values of the event's parameters.

    In summary, you can use web3.js or Ether.js to read events emitted by a smart contract. Both libraries allow you to filter the events by block number and retrieve the events by event name. The main difference is the syntax, but the idea is the same, retrieve events emitted by a smart contract.

    It's also important to note that, both web3.js and Ether.js allows you to listen to events, so you can trigger an action when the event is emitted. This can be useful for creating dapps that respond to events in real-time.

item.title: 了解 Nutanix AHV 以及对 Nutanix 备份的需求 | Understanding Nutanix AHV and the Need for Nutanix Backup
item.description:
它建立在与 Nutanix AOS 相同的基础上，提供了一个可以在云端和本地管理应用程序的环境。 然而，任何技术或应用程序都需要备份解决方案。 通过利用 Nutanix 的 Prism 和 Acropolis 等先进技术，Nutanix Backup 可以轻松监控和保护在 AHV 上运行的主机。

此外，管理员可以从 Prism 界面快速查看失败作业列表，以及从 Nutanix 环境内部和外部使用第三方工具启动恢复会话。

总体而言，Nutanix AHV 为用户提供了一个高效可靠的平台来运行企业工作负载。 通过同时支持 Nutanix Backup 解决方案，组织可以确保其数据始终受到保护，同时仍然享受 Nutanix AHV 的强大功能和灵活性。 由于其复杂性，大型企业基础设施可能难以管理。 当您需要确保位于不同地方的许多数据中心顺畅有效地协同工作时，这个问题就显得尤为重要。 超融合有助于解决此问题。

超融合基础架构解决方案的市场领导者之一 Nutanix 在本文的内容、原因和方式部分进行了讨论。

什么是 Nutanix

自 2009 年 Nutanix 成立以来，该公司已转型为知名虚拟化供应商，帮助客户创建可扩展、灵活且高效的数据中心。 其虚拟化产品 Nutanix AHV 依赖的是超融合基础架构 (HCI) 所依赖的技术。

什么是超融合基础设施 (HCI)？

超融合基础设施 (HCI) 是指一种将存储、计算和网络组件组合到单个预配置系统中的 IT 架构。 它旨在简化和改进 IT 运营，因为它消除了对单独元素的需求。

为什么需要 Nutanix 备份

这就是 Nutanix 备份解决方案的用武之地，它通过异地或本地复制关键数据，同时提供对备份的持续监控，提供全面的数据保护方法。 此外，管理员可以从 Prism 界面快速查看失败作业列表，并从 Nutanix 环境内部和外部使用第三方工具启动恢复会话。

其中包括用于创建异地或本地存储的每日备份的自动化过程，在恢复期间自动将备份映像文件安装到已安装的驱动器，以及根据用户要求安排备份。

总之，Nutanix AHV 为用户提供了一个高效可靠的平台来运行企业工作负载。 通过同时支持 Nutanix Backup 解决方案，组织可以确保其数据始终受到保护，同时仍然享受 Nutanix AHV 的强大功能和灵活性。 请务必牢记，并非所有备份解决方案都是生来平等的，只有使用 Nutanix Backup，组织才能确保完整的数据保护以及在需要时快速恢复。 Working together, Nutanix AHV and Backup provide a powerful combination that can help enterprises achieve their business goals more efficiently and quickly. Nutanix AHV 和 Backup 的协同工作提供了一个强大的组合，可以帮助企业更高效、更快速地实现其业务目标。



原文:

Nutanix AHV (Acropolis Hypervisor) is a powerful, secure, and easy-to-use hypervisor designed for enterprise workloads. It is built on the same foundation as Nutanix AOS and provides an environment where applications can be managed both in the cloud and on-premises. Nutanix AHV also offers robust scalability, allowing administrators to rapidly provision resources such as storage capacity and RAM.

However, with any technology or application comes the need for backup solutions. Nutanix backup solutions provide a comprehensive approach to data protection by replicating critical data offsite or locally while providing continuous monitoring of backups and lightning-fast restores when needed. By leveraging Nutanix’s advanced technologies like Prism and Acropolis, Nutanix Backup can easily monitor and protect hosts running on AHV.

Other features of Nutanix backup include automated processes for creating daily backups that are stored offsite or locally, automatically mounting the backup image files to a mounted drive during restores, and scheduling backups based on user requirements. Additionally, administrators can quickly review a list of failed jobs from the Prism interface, as well as initiate restore sessions from both within the Nutanix environment as well as outside with third-party tools.

Overall, Nutanix AHV provides users with an efficient and reliable platform for running enterprise workloads. By supporting Nutanix Backup solutions alongside it, organizations can ensure their data is protected at all times while still enjoying the power and flexibility of Nutanix AHV. Nutanix Backup is a must-have solution for any organization leveraging Nutanix technology.

Because of their complexity, large enterprise infrastructures can be difficult to manage. When you need to make sure that many data centers located in various places function together smoothly and effectively, the issue is especially pertinent. This issue is helped by hyper-convergence.

One of the market leaders for hyper-converged infrastructure solutions, Nutanix, is discussed in this post's what, why, and how sections.

What Nutanix Is

Since 2009 when Nutanix was founded, the company has transformed into a well-known virtualization vendor enabling clients to create scalable, flexible, and efficient data centers. Nutanix is known for its software-defined storage and cloud computing services. Its virtualization product, Nutanix AHV, relies on Hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) is the technology that relies on.

What Is Hyperсonverged Infrastructure (HCI)?

Hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) refers to a type of IT architecture that combines the storage, computing, and networking components into a single pre-configured system. It is designed for simplifying and improving IT operations as it eliminates the need for separate elements.

Why Nutanix Backup Is Needed

Nutanix AHV can offer efficient management of enterprise workloads but there is still an element of risk associated with these backups in case something goes wrong. This is where Nutanix Backup solutions come in to provide a comprehensive approach to data protection by replicating critical data offsite or locally while providing continuous monitoring of backups. Additionally, administrators can quickly review a list of failed jobs from the Prism interface and initiate restore sessions from both within the Nutanix environment as well as outside with third-party tools.

How Nutanix Backup Works

Nutanix Backup offers a variety of features designed to protect data and provide fast recoveries when needed. These include automated processes for creating daily backups that are stored offsite or locally, automatically mounting the backup image files to a mounted drive during restores, and scheduling backups based on user requirements.

In conclusion, Nutanix AHV provides users with an efficient and reliable platform for running enterprise workloads. By supporting Nutanix Backup solutions alongside it, organizations can ensure their data is protected at all times while still enjoying the power and flexibility of Nutanix AHV. Nutanix Backup is a must-have solution for any organization leveraging Nutanix technology.

It is especially important to keep in mind that not all backup solutions are created equal and only with Nutanix Backup can organizations ensure complete data protection as well as fast recovery when it’s needed. Working together, Nutanix AHV and Backup provide a powerful combination that can help enterprises achieve their business goals more efficiently and quickly.
